message:
bench(integration_regression): rename comparison axis to cass-owned paths

The integration_regression bench suite previously compared
"SqliteStorage (rusqlite) vs FrankenStorage (frankensqlite)" — but
SqliteStorage is now a compatibility alias for FrankenStorage, so both
sides of the comparison were exercising the same engine through
different cass-side wrappers. The bench was still useful (the two
wrappers really do hit different cass code paths) but the labels were
misleading.

Rename the comparison so the bench accurately describes what it
measures:

- setup_sqlite_db -> setup_persist_seeded_db: seeds a database through
  the normalized persist_conversation pipeline (adapter + storage +
  lexical index).
- setup_franken_db -> setup_direct_seeded_db: seeds a database through
  direct FrankenStorage calls only (pre-resolved IDs, storage-only
  writes).

Apply the same renaming to:
- group ids: sqlite_*, franken_* -> persist_seeded_*, direct_seeded_*
- bench_id labels in bench_with_input: "sqlite"/"franken" ->
  "persist"/"direct"
- temp file names: sqlite.db/franken.db -> persist.db/direct.db
- module-level doc comment now states the cass-owned-paths rationale
  and notes that engine-level rusqlite-vs-frankensqlite comparison
  needs a separate harness.

Two bench cleanups landed alongside the rename:

- bench_db_open_comparison: the empty-DB sub-bench previously opened
  one DB per "engine"; collapse to a single empty_db sample since both
  paths produce the same artifact.
- bench_query_comparison: the franken side previously fell back to a
  raw query for the conversation id and skipped franken_list_convs_50
  because of an ORDER BY x IS NULL gap. That gap is gone, so:
  * direct_seeded_list_convs_50 is restored
  * direct_conv_id is now resolved via list_conversations(1, 0) (no
    raw fallback, no RowExt usage — `use frankensqlite::compat::RowExt`
    is dropped)
  * count_sessions_in_range stays on the persist-seeded dataset; the
    direct side keeps a raw COUNT(*) reference labeled
    direct_seeded_raw_count to give a storage-floor comparison.

Drop the now-unused SqliteStorage import; the type still exists as a
re-export elsewhere for callers that want the legacy name.

This is benchmark plumbing only — no production storage or query
behavior changes.
